title: rubus tereticaulis var. derasifolius
intro: Rubus tereticaulis var. derasifolius, also known as the Japanese black thimbleberry or black thimbleberry, is a lesser-known species of thimbleberry, a type of deciduous shrub native to East Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits associated with Rubus tereticaulis var. derasifolius include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is prepared by drying the leaves or stems, then using them to make teas, infusions, or decoctions for various health applications.
